ref.pp:
/-!
# The `eval%` term elaborator

This file provides the `eval% x` term elaborator, which evaluates the constant `x` at compile-time
in the interpreter, and interpolates it into the expression.
-/

/--
`eval% x` evaluates the term `x : X` in the interpreter, and then injects the resulting expression.

As an example:
```lean
example : 2^10 = eval% 2^10 := by
  -- goal is `2^10 = 1024`
  sorry
```
This only works if a `Lean.ToExpr X` instance is available.

Tip: you can use `show_term eval% x` to see the value of `eval% x`.
-/
 @[  term_parser  1000  ]  public  meta  def eval_expr :  Lean.ParserDescr  :=  ParserDescr.node  `Mathlib.Meta.eval_expr  1022  ParserDescr.binary  `andthen  ParserDescr.symbol  "eval% "  ParserDescr.cat  `term  0

expanded.pp:
/--
`eval% x` evaluates the term `x : X` in the interpreter, and then injects the resulting expression.

As an example:
```lean
example : 2^10 = eval% 2^10 := by
  -- goal is `2^10 = 1024`
  sorry
```
This only works if a `Lean.ToExpr X` instance is available.

Tip: you can use `show_term eval% x` to see the value of `eval% x`.
-/
 @[  term_parser  1000  ]  public  meta  def eval_expr :  Lean.ParserDescr  :=  ParserDescr.node  `Mathlib.Meta.eval_expr  1022  ParserDescr.binary  `andthen  ParserDescr.symbol  "eval% "  ParserDescr.cat  `term  0

/--
`eval% x` evaluates the term `x : X` in the interpreter, and then injects the resulting expression.

As an example:
```lean
example : 2^10 = eval% 2^10 := by
  -- goal is `2^10 = 1024`
  sorry
```
This only works if a `Lean.ToExpr X` instance is available.

Tip: you can use `show_term eval% x` to see the value of `eval% x`.
-/
syntax (name := eval_expr) "eval% " term : term

/--
`eval% x` evaluates the term `x : X` in the interpreter, and then injects the resulting expression.

As an example:
```lean
example : 2^10 = eval% 2^10 := by
  -- goal is `2^10 = 1024`
  sorry
```
This only works if a `Lean.ToExpr X` instance is available.

Tip: you can use `show_term eval% x` to see the value of `eval% x`.
-/
syntax (name := eval_expr) "eval% " term : term

clear%  x  ; fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]

have stx :=  @  TSyntax.mk  List.cons._@._internal._hyg.0  `term  List.nil._@._internal._hyg.0  __discr  ;  clear%  x  ; fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)

have  __discr  :=  Syntax.getArg  __discr  1  ;  have stx :=  @  TSyntax.mk  List.cons._@._internal._hyg.0  `term  List.nil._@._internal._hyg.0  __discr  ;  clear%  x  ; fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)

have  __discr  :=  Syntax.getArg  __discr  0  ;  have  __discr  :=  Syntax.getArg  __discr  1  ;  have stx :=  @  TSyntax.mk  List.cons._@._internal._hyg.0  `term  List.nil._@._internal._hyg.0  __discr  ;  clear%  x  ; fun exp => do

match  @  x  with | `(term| eval% $stx) =>  clear%  x  ; fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)
| _ =>  clear%  x  ; fun _ => Elab.throwUnsupportedSyntax

  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)
| _ =>  clear%  x  ; fun _ => Elab.throwUnsupportedSyntax

  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)
| _ =>  clear%  x  ; fun _ => Elab.throwUnsupportedSyntax

@  fun  x  =>  match  @  x  with | `(term| eval% $stx) =>  clear%  x  ; fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)
| _ =>  clear%  x  ; fun _ => Elab.throwUnsupportedSyntax

@  fun  x  =>  match  @  x  with | `(term| eval% $stx) =>  clear%  x  ; fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)
| _ =>  clear%  x  ; fun _ => Elab.throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[term_elab eval_expr, inherit_doc eval_expr]
unsafe def elabEvalExpr : Lean.Elab.Term.TermElab
| `(term| eval% $stx) => fun exp => do
  let e ← Lean.Elab.Term.elabTermAndSynthesize stx exp
  let e ← instantiateMVars e
  let ee ← Meta.mkAppM ``Lean.toExpr #[e]
  Lean.Meta.evalExpr Expr q(Expr) ee (safety := .unsafe)
| _ => fun _ => Elab.throwUnsupportedSyntax
